pr.title: prep makefile for multi-compiler usage and organize some sysdolphin headers
pr.author: EpochFlame

summary: Small infrastructure/header-organization PR that moved sysdolphin baselib id.h and objalloc.h from src/sysdolphin/baselib into include/sysdolphin for easier testing, updated all affected includes to use sysdolphin/id.h or sysdolphin/objalloc.h, adjusted include guards to namespaced forms, and made minor build/gitignore changes for multi-compiler or Windows-tooling workflows. The PR was closed without merge and has no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Updated local includes of id.h and objalloc.h to qualified include paths such as "sysdolphin/id.h" and "sysdolphin/objalloc.h".
Changed id.h include guard from _id_h_ to _sysdolphin_id_h_.
Changed objalloc.h include guard from _objalloc_h_ to _sysdolphin_objalloc_h_.
Changed the Makefile CC assignment from immediate assignment ':=' to recursive assignment '=' for the mwcceppc.exe command.
Added root .gitignore entries for generated Windows/tooling artifacts: *.exe, *.dll, and *.bat.
Added include/.gitignore to ignore *.s files under include.
smart_moves: Moved reusable sysdolphin headers into include/sysdolphin instead of leaving them beside baselib source files, matching the PR body's stated goal that headers should be in include for ease of testing.
Used qualified include paths after moving headers, reducing ambiguity between local baselib headers and public include-tree headers.
Namespaced include guards with sysdolphin to avoid generic guard names like _id_h_ and _objalloc_h_ that could collide as more headers are organized.
Kept the source-impact minimal: most changes are one-line include updates, making the refactor easy to inspect.
Ignored generated compiler/tooling files that could appear during Windows or Wine-based multi-compiler workflows.
decomp_lessons: When promoting a header from a source-local directory to the shared include tree, update dependents to include it via its intended public path, e.g. "sysdolphin/objalloc.h", rather than relying on incidental local include search order.
Use subsystem-qualified include guards for shared headers; generic guards are especially risky in a growing decompilation tree with many similarly named SDK-like headers.

Small build-system preparation changes should be isolated from matching-sensitive code changes when possible; this PR mostly limits itself to include paths, guards, gitignore entries, and a Makefile assignment form.
PR body evidence says the motivation was testing convenience: public or test-consumed headers may need to live under include even if their original implementation sits under src/sysdolphin/baselib.
Because this PR was closed without merge in the provided data, future work should verify whether the same header relocation/build changes landed elsewhere before reusing it directly.
naming_conventions: Shared sysdolphin headers are placed under include/sysdolphin/ and included as "sysdolphin/<name>.h".
Header guards for sysdolphin include-tree headers use subsystem-qualified names such as _sysdolphin_id_h_ and _sysdolphin_objalloc_h_.
The diff preserves existing HSD naming seen in declarations and macros, including HSD_A_OP_NONE, HSD_A_OP_CON, HSD_ObjAllocData, HSD_RenderInitAllocData, and HSD_RenderGetAllocData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct function matching, assembly scheduling, or codegen tactic is evidenced in the slice.
The new include/.gitignore entry for *.s suggests generated assembly files under include may appear during testing or compiler workflows, but the slice does not explain the exact workflow.
Changing Makefile CC from ':=' to '=' may support later or variable-dependent compiler selection for multi-compiler usage, but the provided diff does not document the full intended mechanism.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records are present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so there is no evidence here of acceptance or requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that moved sysdolphin id.h or objalloc.h into include/sysdolphin and check whether this closed PR was superseded.
Search Makefile history for multi-compiler support and the rationale for using recursive '=' versus immediate ':=' assignment for CC.
Search for review or commit history around include/.gitignore and generated *.s files to understand the testing workflow referenced by the PR body.
Check whether generic include guards _id_h_ or _objalloc_h_ remained elsewhere after this PR was closed.
